channel.title: Home on Semantastic CSS
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Semantastic CSS

item.title: Tokens
item.description:
Foundation Layer

Design Tokens

The foundational design values that power the entire Semantastic system.

Every system should have tokens. Tokens are units of common values to make sure there is a sense of conisetency to the site. Large text is the same size across the site, spacing is consistent. Make your users brain happy by allowing them to understand where they should expect content to be sitting in relation to one another.

item.title: Colors
item.description:
Make It pretty

Colors

we want to be semantic, doesn't me we can't look good.

Modern CSS has delightful tools for working with color. colors.css leverages OKLCH and color-mix to make good and consistent colors that only need a small handful swapped out to theme the site. Dark mode included.

colors.css has many colors defined. But less than a dozen core colors need to be defined to totally customize the looks.

Why OKLCH?

Traditional HSL is not perceptually uniform. Colors with the same lightness value can appear dramatically different in brightness:

item.title: Layouts
item.description:
Every Thing in The Right Place

Layout Primatives

Custom tags inspired by Every Layout.

A collection of custom tags for layout elements to control the flow of your page. Based on Every Layout the goal is to accept that your website is not a poster and instead build primitives that make sure your sites content flows correctly regardless of device size.

Custom tags are used because div soup is bad. HTML allows you to name your own tags, so why not leverage it? often you'll see class="foo" instead. But what if Bar is a class as well? class="foo bar" may indeed be nonsensical. As such enforcing the layouts as distinct elements creates the nicest experience in my opinion.

item.title: Components
item.description:
Pure CSS

Components

Interactive components built with CSS only — no JavaScript required.

Accessible, keyboard-navigable, and configurable with custom properties.

<ui-tabs> — Tab Navigation

Pure CSS tabs using radio inputs and labels. Three variants: default (bordered), pills, and underline. Implemented as a custom element for cleaner markup.

Structure

<ui-tabs>
 <input type="radio" name="demo" id="tab1" checked>
 <label for="tab1">Tab 1</label>
 <div data-content>Content 1</div>

 <input type="radio" name="demo" id="tab2">
 <label for="tab2">Tab 2</label>
 <div data-content>Content 2</div>
</ui-tabs>
 Properties --tab-bg transparent --tab-bg-active var(--color-primary) --tab-color #666 --tab-color-active var(--color-canvas) --tab-border-color var(--color-border) --tab-border-width 2px --tab-padding 0.75rem 1.5rem --tab-radius 6px 6px 0 0

Default Tabs

Overview

This is the overview panel. Tabs use radio inputs under the hood, so only one can be active at a time.

item.title: Candy
item.description:
Optional CSS

Candy Components

Production-ready components built on Semantastic design system primitives.

candy.css is an optional, higher-level CSS layer that provides 15 components implemented as custom elements — replacing verbose inline styles with clean, semantic markup.

Note: This file is completely optional. The Semantastic system works perfectly without it. Candy.css is the only CSS file in the system that imports dependencies.

Cards & Containers

Base Card

Simple card with rounded corners, border, and padding. Implemented as a custom element.
